# yii3-feature-flags-db

## Install

```bash
composer require rasuvaeff/yii3-feature-flags-db
```

## Rules

- Implements `Rasuvaeff\Yii3FeatureFlags\WritableFlagProvider` (which `extends FlagProvider`).
- `getFlags()` returns `array<string, Flag>`; one query via yiisoft/db `Query` builder.
- `save(Flag)` is an upsert keyed by `name`; `remove(string)` is idempotent.
- Invalid row shape / out-of-range rollout / invalid name throws `InvalidFlagRowException` (never silently skips; core errors wrapped).
- `CachedFlagProvider` is a PSR-16 decorator; cache read/write failure is non-fatal.
- `CachedFlagProvider` write-through: after `save()`/`remove()` it calls `clear()` before returning. When inner is read-only, writes are no-ops.
- `salt === name` is serialised as `''` on write so the round-trip preserves `emptySaltFallsBackToName`.
- Kill switch, rollout hash, environment targeting — all remain in core.
- Ships a `yiisoft/db-migration` migration in `migrations/`; table name is a migration constructor arg (default `feature_flags`).
- `config/di.php` binds `WritableFlagProvider` to the same instance as `FlagProvider` via `Yiisoft\Definitions\Reference`.

## Database schema

Create the table via the shipped migration (register `migrations/` in
`yiisoft/db-migration` `sourcePaths`, run `./yii migrate:up`), or manually:

```sql
CREATE TABLE feature_flags (
    name        VARCHAR(190) PRIMARY KEY,
    enabled     BOOLEAN      NOT NULL DEFAULT TRUE,
    salt        VARCHAR(190) NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
    rollout     SMALLINT     NOT NULL DEFAULT 100,
    kill_switch BOOLEAN      NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE,
    environments TEXT        NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]'
);
```

## API

### DbFlagProvider

```php
use Rasuvaeff\Yii3FeatureFlagsDb\DbFlagProvider;

$provider = new DbFlagProvider(
    db: $connection,            // Yiisoft\Db\Connection\ConnectionInterface
    table: 'feature_flags',     // optional, default 'feature_flags'
);

$flags = $provider->getFlags();  // array<string, Flag>
$provider->save(flag: $flag);    // upsert by name
$provider->remove(name: 'old');  // idempotent delete by name
```

### CachedFlagProvider

```php
use Rasuvaeff\Yii3FeatureFlagsDb\CachedFlagProvider;

$cached = new CachedFlagProvider(
    inner: $dbProvider,          // FlagProvider (WritableFlagProvider for writes)
    cache: $psr16,               // Psr\SimpleCache\CacheInterface
    ttl: 60,                     // int<0, max> seconds
);

$flags = $cached->getFlags();    // from cache or inner
$cached->save(flag: $flag);      // delegates when inner is WritableFlagProvider, then clear()
$cached->remove(name: 'old');    // delegates when inner is WritableFlagProvider, then clear()
$cached->clear();                // invalidate cache
```

### FlagRowMapper

```php
// @internal — unit-tested directly, but not part of the public contract.
use Rasuvaeff\Yii3FeatureFlagsDb\FlagRowMapper;

$json = FlagRowMapper::encodeEnvironments(environments: ['production', 'staging']);
// => '["production","staging"]'
```

`extractEnvironments()` is the inverse used by `DbFlagProvider::getFlags()`; the
two are round-trip compatible.

### InvalidFlagRowException

```php
use Rasuvaeff\Yii3FeatureFlagsDb\Exception\InvalidFlagRowException;
```

Thrown when a DB row is missing required columns, has wrong types, contains malformed `environments` JSON, or yields an invalid flag (bad name, rollout outside `0..100`). Core validation errors are wrapped into this exception.

### WritableFlagProvider DI binding

```php
use Rasuvaeff\Yii3FeatureFlags\FlagProvider;
use Rasuvaeff\Yii3FeatureFlags\WritableFlagProvider;
use Yiisoft\Definitions\Reference;

return [
    FlagProvider::class => /* closure producing DbFlagProvider or CachedFlagProvider */,
    WritableFlagProvider::class => Reference::to(FlagProvider::class),
];
```

Inject `WritableFlagProvider` for writes, `FlagProvider` for reads; both resolve to the same instance.

### Usage with FeatureFlags

```php
use Rasuvaeff\Yii3FeatureFlags\FeatureFlags;
use Rasuvaeff\Yii3FeatureFlagsDb\DbFlagProvider;

$featureFlags = new FeatureFlags(
    provider: new DbFlagProvider(db: $connection),
);

$featureFlags->isEnabled('new-checkout');
```
